_meta.source: PCI DSS v4.0.1 PDF (June 2024), mechanical extraction via pdftotext -layout
_meta.licence: PCI SSC Read and Copy License — internal use; this artifact stores IDs/counts + section titles only

counting_conventions.goals: 6 goal groups in the standard's overview table
counting_conventions.defined_requirements: numbered x.y.z and x.y.z.w rows in the main body (testing procedures excluded — they carry letter suffixes)
counting_conventions.appendix_requirements: A1/A2/A3 numbered rows (sections = single-dot, requirements = deeper)
counting_conventions.future_dated_markers: occurrences of the 'best practice until 31 March 2025' applicability note in v4.0.1 — ALL IN FORCE since 2025-03-31

goals: Build and Maintain a Secure Network and Systems

principal_requirements.1: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
principal_requirements.2: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
principal_requirements.4: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission Over Open, Public Networks
principal_requirements.5: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
principal_requirements.6: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
principal_requirements.7: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
principal_requirements.8: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
principal_requirements.9: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
principal_requirements.10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
principal_requirements.11: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
principal_requirements.12: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

sections.1.1: Processes and mechanisms for installing and maintaining network security controls are defined and understood.
sections.1.2: Network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained.
sections.1.3: Network access to and from the cardholder data environment is restricted.
sections.1.4: Network connections between trusted and untrusted networks are controlled.
sections.1.5: Risks to the CDE from computing devices that are able to connect to both untrusted networks and the CDE are mitigated.
sections.2.1: Processes and mechanisms for applying secure configurations to all system components are defined and understood.
sections.2.2: System components are configured and managed securely.
sections.2.3: Wireless environments are configured and managed securely.
sections.3.1: Processes and mechanisms for protecting stored account data are defined and understood.
sections.3.2: Storage of account data is kept to a minimum.
sections.3.3: Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not stored after authorization.
sections.3.4: Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy PAN are restricted.
sections.3.5: Primary account number (PAN) is secured wherever it is stored.
sections.3.6: Cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data are secured.
sections.3.7: Where cryptography is used to protect stored account data, key management processes and procedures covering all aspects of the key
sections.4.1: Processes and mechanisms for protecting cardholder data with strong cryptography during transmission over open, public networks are
sections.4.2: PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission
sections.5.1: Processes and mechanisms for protecting all systems and networks from malicious software are defined and understood.
sections.5.2: Malicious software (malware) is prevented, or detected and addressed.
sections.5.3: Anti-malware mechanisms and processes are active, maintained, and monitored.
sections.5.4: Anti-phishing mechanisms protect users against phishing attacks.
sections.6.1: Processes and mechanisms for developing and maintaining secure systems and software are defined and understood.
sections.6.2: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely.
sections.6.3: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed.
sections.6.4: Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks.
sections.6.5: Changes to all system components are managed securely.
sections.7.1: Processes and mechanisms for restricting access to system components and cardholder data by business need to know are defined and
sections.7.2: Access to system components and data is appropriately defined and assigned.
sections.7.3: Access to system components and data is managed via an access control system(s).
sections.8.1: Processes and mechanisms for identifying users and authenticating access to system components are defined and understood.
sections.8.2: User identification and related accounts for users and administrators are strictly managed throughout an account’s lifecycle.
sections.8.3: Strong authentication for users and administrators is established and managed.
sections.8.4: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is implemented to secure access into the CDE.
sections.8.5: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) systems are configured to prevent misuse.
sections.8.6: Use of application and system accounts and associated authentication factors is strictly managed.
sections.9.1: Processes and mechanisms for restricting physical access to cardholder data are defined and understood.
sections.9.2: Physical access controls manage entry into facilities and systems containing cardholder data.
sections.9.3: Physical access for personnel and visitors is authorized and managed.
sections.9.4: Media with cardholder data is securely stored, accessed, distributed, and destroyed.
sections.9.5: Point of interaction (POI) devices are protected from tampering and unauthorized substitution.
sections.10.1: Processes and mechanisms for logging and monitoring all access to system components and cardholder data are defined and
sections.10.2: Audit logs are implemented to support the detection of anomalies and suspicious activity, and the forensic analysis of events.
sections.10.3: Audit logs are protected from destruction and unauthorized modifications.
sections.10.4: Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies or suspicious activity.
sections.10.5: Audit log history is retained and available for analysis.
sections.10.6: Time-synchronization mechanisms support consistent time settings across all systems.
sections.10.7: Failures of critical security control systems are detected, reported, and responded to promptly.
sections.11.1: Processes and mechanisms for regularly testing security of systems and networks are defined and understood.
sections.11.2: Wireless access points are identified and monitored, and unauthorized wireless access points are addressed.
sections.11.3: External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed.
sections.11.4: External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed, and exploitable vulnerabilities and security weaknesses are corrected.
sections.11.5: Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected and responded to.
sections.11.6: Unauthorized changes on payment pages are detected and responded to.
sections.12.1: A comprehensive information security policy that governs and provides direction for protection of the entity’s information assets is known
sections.12.2: Acceptable use policies for end-user technologies are defined and implemented.
sections.12.3: Risks to the cardholder data environment are formally identified, evaluated, and managed.
sections.12.5: PCI DSS scope is documented and validated.
sections.12.6: Security awareness education is an ongoing activity.
sections.12.7: Personnel are screened to reduce risks from insider threats.
sections.12.8: Risk to information assets associated with third-party service provider (TPSP) relationships is managed.
sections.12.9: Third-party service providers (TPSPs) support their customers’ PCI DSS compliance.
sections.12.10: Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately.
